modules.title: Abaji Production — Water to Pallet
modules.subtitle: Case-aligned overview from deep-well extraction through treatment, bottling, labeling, packaging, and palletizing.
safetyInsights: Confined-space and slip risks near sumps and drains — follow local permit rules.
Hot surfaces and steam during CIP — verify isolation before opening circuits.
Pinch and shear points on conveyors, labelers, and palletizers — guards are mandatory.
Chemical exposure during sanitation — SDS, PPE, and buddy checks as per site SOP.
Forklift / AGV interaction zones — stay within marked walkways and wear hi-vis.

quiz.question: Why is deep-well extraction monitored before the treatment train?
options: To catch raw-water excursions early and protect downstream barriers
To disable the labeler vision system
quiz.feedbackWrong: Raw-water quality shifts first appear at the source; early detection protects media, membranes, and chemical dosing stability.

quiz.question: Labeling defects most often trace back to:
options: Web alignment, tension control, or vision trigger timing
quiz.feedbackWrong: Label application is sensitive to mechanical alignment and electronic triggers; verify mechanical setup before blaming upstream water.

quiz.question: Before releasing a pallet to logistics, which pairing is most critical?
options: Cap color and shrink oven brand
Case count, case coding, and WMS scan validation
quiz.feedbackWrong: Traceability depends on correct case coding and a clean handoff to the warehouse system of record.

timeline.title: Water Extraction (Deep Well)
timeline.whatIsThis: Groundwater is lifted from permitted deep wells through screened intakes and pumped toward onsite break tanks. This is the boundary where ‘factory water’ begins — before any treatment barrier.
timeline.mechanicalSpecs: Typical assets include submersible or line-shaft pumps, VFD-controlled motors, wellhead flow/totalizing meters, and raw-water analytics (conductivity, turbidity, optional organics scanning). NPSH margins and pump curves are validated against seasonal drawdown.
diagramSteps: Break tank / transfer to treatment header
operationalRisks: Pump cavitation or loss of prime if level controls drift
Screen blinding increasing differential and starving downstream flow
Instrumentation freeze causing untreated diversion — follow alarm response cards

timeline.title: Treatment
timeline.whatIsThis: A multi-barrier train removes particles, adjusts chemistry, and polishes water to the specification required for the SKU family. Each step has validated setpoints tied to HACCP or site equivalent.
timeline.mechanicalSpecs: May include multimedia filters, activated carbon, cartridge guards, membrane racks, UV, and controlled disinfection residual management. Critical instruments: differential pressure, SDI/turbidity trends, membrane flux, and interlock permissives to the filler loop.
diagramSteps: Membrane / UV / residual control → product water header
operationalRisks: Membrane fouling or carbon breakthrough shifting organics
Chemical dosing drift affecting pH/residual
Bypass valves left manual-open after maintenance

timeline.title: Bottling
timeline.whatIsThis: Sterile or hygienic filling places the correct volume into bottles under controlled environment conditions, then applies closures with torque and presence checks.
timeline.mechanicalSpecs: Rotary or linear fillers, HEPA or controlled airflow where applicable, capper with torque feedback, checkweigher, reject tracking, and CIP/SIP interlocks to the MES batch record.
diagramSteps: Reject lane / cause logging in MES
operationalRisks: Fill valve hang-up creating chronic under/over fill
Cap skew causing line stoppages and rework
False rejects if checkweigher drifts out of calibration

timeline.title: Labeling
timeline.whatIsThis: Applies primary labels with correct artwork, registration, and lot coding. Vision systems confirm presence and basic quality attributes at line speed.
timeline.mechanicalSpecs: Unwind tension control, registration marks, peel plate geometry, nip rollers, and vision exposure tuning. Changeovers tie to verified SKU recipes.
diagramSteps: Apply & wrap / spot label per format
operationalRisks: Web breaks from tension spikes
Adhesive cold-flow in winter months affecting lap seams
Wrong artwork loaded — always barcode-scan rolls

timeline.title: Packaging
timeline.whatIsThis: Groups bottles into shippers, applies case coding, and prepares stable unit loads for the palletizer. This is where consumer packs become logistics units.
timeline.mechanicalSpecs: Wraparounds, partition inserters, case erectors, glue or tape systems, inkjet/laser coders with verified contrast, and case squaring before pallet patterns.
operationalRisks: Weak case seals leading to downstream jams
Coder contrast failure → unreadable logistics scans
Partition mis-feed causing product scuffing

timeline.title: Palletizing
timeline.whatIsThis: Stacks cases into approved patterns, applies slip sheets or tier sheets as required, and hands off stretch-wrapped or strapped loads to warehouse traffic.
timeline.mechanicalSpecs: Layer-forming logic, vacuum or clamp end-of-arm tooling, pattern tables per SKU, stretch-wrapper film tension, and AGV / forklift interface scanners.
operationalRisks: Unstable patterns if case dimensions vary
Robot envelope entry while not in teach mode — never bypass light curtains
AGV handshake faults blocking dispatch lanes

modules.title: Abaji Industrial Services
modules.subtitle: Chillers, air handling, compressed air, and effluent treatment — the invisible systems that stabilize every filling hour.
safetyInsights: Lockout/tagout on rotating equipment and electrical isolators before service.
Chemical handling in water treatment / ETP areas — full PPE and spill kits.
Working at height on AHU galleries — harness and barricades per site rules.
Hearing conservation zones around compressors — signage is mandatory.
Confined-space entry to tanks or sumps — permits and gas checks only.

quiz.question: Rising approach temperature on a chiller often suggests:
options: Fouling, non-condensables, or refrigerant charge issues
quiz.feedbackWrong: Heat rejection paths and charge quality dominate chiller stability — trend pressures and subcooling before adjusting setpoints blindly.

quiz.question: Why monitor oil carryover on compressors?
options: It protects air quality for product-contact pneumatics and points of use
quiz.feedbackWrong: Oil aerosols can foul dryers and risk product-contact contamination if coalescing stages are degraded.

timeline.title: Chiller Operations
timeline.whatIsThis: Removes heat from process circuits — often tied to syrup cooling, heat exchangers, or critical HVAC loads. Stable chilled water temperature keeps filling and batching predictable.
timeline.mechanicalSpecs: Evaporator / condenser approach temps, refrigerant circuits, oil management, economizers where fitted, and staging logic across machines. BMS points: CHWS/CHWR, tonnage, kW/ton, alarm shelving rules.
diagramSteps: Condenser heat rejection → tower or dry cooler
operationalRisks: Low flow trips if balance valves drift
Head pressure excursions from tower fouling
Hard starts after power blips without proper restart sequencing

timeline.title: Air Handling Units (AHU)
timeline.whatIsThis: Treats and moves air for hygiene zones, comfort, or equipment cooling. Filtration stages and pressure cascades protect sensitive processes.
timeline.mechanicalSpecs: Supply/return fans with VFDs, heating/cooling coils, filters (G4–H13 depending on zone), humidification if installed, and room pressure monitors tied to alarms.
operationalRisks: Clogged filters increasing fan energy and starving airflow
Frozen coils if glycol strength or valves fail
Loss of cascade pressure risking cross-contamination paths

timeline.title: Compressors
timeline.whatIsThis: Generates plant air for pneumatics, actuators, and sometimes instrument air after appropriate drying and filtration.
timeline.mechanicalSpecs: Oil-free or treated oil-flooded compressors, intercoolers, desiccant or refrigerant dryers, coalescing filters, dew point monitors, and receiver sizing for demand events.
operationalRisks: High dew point if dryer towers fail to switch
Undersized receiver causing pressure sag and filler faults
Leaks — the largest hidden energy thief

timeline.title: Effluent Treatment
timeline.whatIsThis: Treats process and sanitary effluent prior to discharge under permit. Protects community watercourses and keeps the site within regulatory consent.
timeline.mechanicalSpecs: Equalization, pH control, DAF or clarification as designed, biological reactors, final polishing, sludge handling, and online parameter logging for compliance reporting.
operationalRisks: Shock loads from CIP batches affecting biology
Sensor drift causing false compliance readings
Chemical storage incompatibility — segregation is non-negotiable

modules.title: Safety Systems & Field Discipline
modules.subtitle: Interlocks, stops, and behaviors that keep teams safe around motion, energy, and chemistry.
safetyInsights: Never defeat a guard or interlock — use the management of change process.
LOTO is personal verification, not a tag on a handle you did not isolate.
E-stops are for emergencies, not production pacing.
Energy isolation includes pneumatic and stored mechanical energy.
Report near-misses; they are free lessons before harm occurs.

quiz.question: A light curtain is muted during a setup task. Who must authorize and how is risk reduced?
options: Anyone on shift — no extra controls
Authorized person under a defined safe setup procedure with reduced speed and restricted access
Mute is always illegal — never mind context
quiz.feedbackWrong: Muting is sometimes engineered for setup — it must follow written procedures, training, and reduced risk states.

quiz.question: Permissive logic in a PLC / safety PLC primarily ensures:
options: Motion only occurs when required safe states are proven
quiz.feedbackWrong: Permissives prove guards closed, air present, alignment complete, etc., before energizing motion.

timeline.title: Hazard Identification
timeline.whatIsThis: A structured look at what can hurt people: motion, hot surfaces, stored energy, chemicals, noise, and human factors. Hazards are recorded before controls are chosen.
timeline.mechanicalSpecs: Methodologies aligned to ISO 12100-style risk assessment, severity × exposure matrices, and verification against PL/SIL targets where machinery directives apply.
operationalRisks: Undocumented tasks after line modifications
Assuming ‘common sense’ replaces assessment

timeline.title: Guarding & Access Control
timeline.whatIsThis: Physical and electronic barriers that prevent reach into danger zones while production runs. Access doors use interlocks rather than ‘clever workarounds’.
timeline.mechanicalSpecs: RFID keys, tongue interlocks, coded magnets, safety-rated relays, and reset policies that prevent automatic restart after breach.
diagramSteps: Teach / setup modes with reduced risk
operationalRisks: Cable-tied interlocks defeated ‘just for a minute’
Unverified spare parts changing switch operating distances

timeline.title: Emergency Stop Philosophy
timeline.whatIsThis: Red mushroom devices that command a stop category designed into the machine. They exist to reduce harm when something goes wrong — not for coffee breaks.
timeline.mechanicalSpecs: Category 0/1 behavior per machine standard, monitored dual-channel chains, safe torque-off on drives, and time-stamped event capture in diagnostics.
diagramSteps: Require intentional reset — no auto restart
operationalRisks: Confusing e-stop with normal stop causing nuisance trips
Reset without root-cause check

timeline.title: LOTO & Field Execution
timeline.whatIsThis: Lockout/tagout proves zero energy before hands enter the line. Each worker applies their own lock where they personally verified isolation.
timeline.mechanicalSpecs: Written procedures per asset, step-by-step verification (voltage testers, try-out), group lock boxes, and shift transfer rules.
operationalRisks: Adjacent lines left running without barriers
Rushing during night shift turnover
